snippets.title: Manual naive Bayes score with log probabilities
snippets.explanation: Log-domain scoring implements argmax_y [log P(y) + Σᵢ log P(xᵢ|y)]. This is the core prediction step shared by all Naive Bayes variants.

snippets.title: sklearn GaussianNB — independence in action
snippets.code:
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB

X, y = load_iris(return_X_y=True)
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=0)

clf = GaussianNB()
clf.fit(X_train, y_train)

# Internally: per-class mean/var per feature, then product of Gaussian densities
print(clf.predict(X_test[:3]))
print(clf.predict_proba(X_test[:3]))
snippets.explanation: GaussianNB estimates P(xᵢ|y) as a univariate normal per (feature, class) and multiplies them—exactly the conditional independence assumption with Gaussian factors.

snippets.title: Compare joint vs naive likelihood on toy counts
snippets.code:
# Toy counts for (FREE, !!!) under spam — 100 emails
joint_spam = {(1, 1): 35, (1, 0): 25, (0, 1): 20, (0, 0): 20}
n = sum(joint_spam.values())

# Joint estimate P(x|spam)
p_joint_11 = joint_spam[(1, 1)] / n

# Naive estimate: P(FREE=1|spam) * P(!!!=1|spam)
p_free = (joint_spam[(1, 1)] + joint_spam[(1, 0)]) / n
p_bangs = (joint_spam[(1, 1)] + joint_spam[(0, 1)]) / n
p_naive_11 = p_free * p_bangs

print(f"joint P(1,1|spam) = {p_joint_11:.3f}")
print(f"naive P(1,1|spam) = {p_naive_11:.3f}")
snippets.explanation: When features co-occur differently than independence predicts, joint and naive estimates diverge—showing where the assumption helps (fewer parameters) vs hurts (bias).
